info.title: Exberry Admin API
info.description:
Exberry Admin API allows managing exchange static data (instruments, trading calendars and more) as well as sending operational commands (EOD, halt trading and more).

**Sandbox environment endpoint:** `https://admin-api.uat.exberry-uat.io`

**Guidelines:**
- All numbers are stringified unless explicitly mentioned otherwise. Stringified integers are marked with pattern `^-?[0-9]+$`; stringified decimals with `format: decimal`.
- Optional fields should be omitted from request if not required
- System ignores any additional parameter that are sent on request body but was not specified in this document

**HTTP Error Codes:**
- 400: Maximum request size exceeded (60KB) or Invalid JSON
- 401: Invalid token
- 403: Authentication and authorization errors
- 404: Route not found
- 500: Any other error
- 503: System not available
- 504: Timeout

URL_ORIGIN.description: Base URL for the Admin API

BearerAuth.description: JWT token obtained from the /api/auth/token endpoint

id.description: Account ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Account)

name.description:
Account Name 
Max length = 50 and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

mpId.description: MP ID (System generated identifier of MPs)

status.description:
Active — activity through the account is allowed
Blocked — activity through the account is not allowed

id.description: Calendar ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Calendar)

tradingDays.description: List of days in week that trading is allowed

name.description: Unique Calendar name

timeZone.description:
Time zone difference from UTC. This affects all the dates and times in the calendar 
samples: "+03:00", "-02:00"

marketClose.description: End trading hour, mandatory in case marketOpen is sent

name.description: Holiday name

trigger.description:
When the auction is triggered.
TimeBased — default)- for regular auction schedule that is based on date and time (you can have many auctions with that trigger
Resume — for auctions that will be triggered as a result of manual resume operation by system operator who will click “Resume” button, or by scheduled resume (you can have only one auction per calendar with that trigger
AutoResume — for auctions that will be triggered as a result of automatic resume operation by system as part of CBR. This can happen in case of a temporary halt that is being configured in Circuit Breaker Rule. (you can have only one auction per calendar with that trigger

days.description:
List of days in week that auctions will start 
Mandatory if trigger = TimeBased

startTimes.description:
List of start time for the auction in terms of calendar time zone 
Mandatory if trigger = TimeBased 
Auction objects are sorted by ascending order of this field.

allowedTimeInForces.description:
List of allowed TIF . 
Note: IOC behaves like GAA but allows the use of market orders during the auction. 
Refer here for more details

matchingAlgorithm.description:
Auction matching algorithm,
PRICE_AND_TIME — execution based on price and time priority
EQUILIBRIUM_PRICE — execution based on single calculated equilibrium price
EQUILIBRIUM_PRICE_2 — execution based on single calculated equilibrium price

eventsModes.description:
List of events to be published during auction in `orderBookDepth`:
LIT — regular order events
INDICATIVE_PRICE — AuctionIndicativeEP events

randomEndTime.description:
Auction random end time in milliseconds. Time range in which auction ends. If specified, auction ends randomly in time range [X - randomEndTime / 2; X + randomEndTime / 2] 
X = startTime + duration

crossingPause.description: Auction crossing pause is a time range which will delay the crossing(executions) time from start in milliseconds. During this time - no orders commands can be sent.

overrideReferencePrice.description:
Whether to refer to the auction equilibrium price as refrence price to daily price bands. 
relevant only when matchingAlgorithm = `EQUILIBRIUM_PRICE` 
Note: empty means false.

criticalPhase.description: Critical phase in milliseconds where auction will be checked if needs to be extended

timeFrom.description: Specify the extension time start in milliseconds

timeTo.description:
Specify the extension time end in milliseconds 
In case timeTo > timeFrom , extension will be randomly selected within that range (include timeTo & timeFrom)

id.description: CBR ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update CBR)

name.description: Unique CBR name

instrumentIds.description:
List of instrument ids to apply this rule 
If empty - apply this rule for all instruments

instrumentGroupIds.description:
List of instrument group ids to apply this rule 
Note: Only one of instrumentIds and instrumentGroupIds is allowed

status.description:
ACTIVE — CBR is active
DISABLED — CBR is not active

type.description:
MARKET_RATE — monitor market rate changes since the previous day's close rate or daily settlement price if exists.
DYNAMIC_MARKET_RATE — monitor market rate changes since the previous trade rate (last price
MARKET_RATE_POTENTIAL_EXECUTION — monitor potential executions that may impact market rate changes since the previous day's close rate or daily settlement price if exists.
DYNAMIC_MARKET_RATE_POTENTIAL_EXECUTION — monitor potential executions that may impact market rate changes since the previous trade rate (last price

value.description:
The value in percent that considered breach (value > 1 means increase on rate and 0 < value < 1 means decline rate) Example: 
0.8 means decline of 20% 
1.2 means increase of 20%

actionType.description:
HALT_TRADING — halt the trading for the instrument
NOTIFY_BACKOFFICE — send notification to Admin users
RESUME_AUCTION — will trigger the auto_resume_auction

actionValue.description:
“HALT_TRADING” - If empty - halt the trading for that instrument permanently (can only be resumed manually) Else time in minutes. 
“NOTIFY_BACKOFFICE” - always empty 
`NEW`“RESUME_AUCTION” - always empty

priority.description: Rule priority - the highest is more important.

id.description: Instrument ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Instrument)

symbol.description: Unique instrument symbol. Max length 16. Allowed characters: a-Z, 0-9, - . ^ = /

activityStatus.description:
ACTIVE — trading allowed
DISABLED — Trading not allowed
ARCHIVED — Can't be used in Create/ Update APIs but only in the Get APIs, for more details see here

maxDepth.description: Maximum allowed depth (for both buy and sell) for the instrument

maxOrderValue.description: Maximum allowed order value for the instrument in quote currency terms

maxPrice.description: Maximum allowed price for the instrument

minPrice.description: Minimum allowed price for the instrument

quantityIncrement.description: Order quantity must be multiplied by the quantity increment

groupIds.description:
Optional 
A list of group Ids.

startDate.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) 
Used for derivatives, trading is allowed from this time. 
Before this time, marketStatus is in Closed state without changing as per the calendar.

stopDate.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) 
Used for derivatives, trading is allowed up to this time. 
At this time all active orders are being cancelled. 
See tradingCutoffs for instrument expiration details 
At this time marketStatus changes to Closed and remains in the same state without changing as per the calendar.

type.description:
One of the below values: 
Refer Trade Entry/type field for the values; all values are supported.

timestamp.description:
Cutoff time for trade reporting of the type defined in the ‘type’ field. 
Instrument status changed to be Disabled at the latest cutoff. (considering also the stopDate) 
If not defined, stopDate will be the cutoff time for trade types not defined. 
Applicable to any Trade entry

tradingCutoffs.description: list of trading cutoffs for Trade Entry trade types

deliveryType.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) 
Delivery type
P — Physical
C — Cash
N — Non-Deliverable
X — Not set

contractSize.description:
Optional 
Contract size 
`(NEW v1.54.0)` 
The multiplier used to convert instrument trading units to Settlement units (i.e. asset traded in kg and settled in grams) 
If not set, treated as contractSize = 1

contractSizeUnit.description:
Optional 
Specifies the units that instrument is trading in.

priceMultiplier.description:
Optional 
The multiplier used to convert quoted per units to Settlement units 
For example, GOLD 99.99% quoted in USD per oz. price multiplier is applicable to “per oz” part of the definition. 
If not set, treated as priceMultiplier = 1

priceUnit.description:
Optional 
Specifies the quoted unit of the instrument. For example, the price is quoted per Oz.

expiryDate.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) 
Expiry date. format YYYY-MM-DD

strike.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) 
Option strike

exerciseType.description:
Mandatory if Category = O (option) 
Option exercise type
A — American
E — European
B — Bermudan

dailyMinPricePercentage.description: Minimum allowed price % change from the last reference price for the instrument

dailyMaxPricePercentage.description: Maximum allowed price % change from the last reference price for the instrument

dailyMinPriceAbsolute.description:
Minimum allowed absolute price change from the reference price for the instrument 
Cannot be used together with `dailyMinPricePercentage`

dailyMaxPriceAbsolute.description:
Maximum allowed absolute price change from the reference price for the instrument 
Cannot be used together with `dailyMaxPricePercentage`

tickMinPricePercentage.description: Minimum allowed price % change from the last executed price for the instrument

tickMaxPricePercentage.description: Maximum allowed price % change from the last executed price for the instrument

tickMinPriceAbsolute.description:
Minimum allowed absolute price change from the tick reference price 
Cannot be used together with `tickMinPricePercentage`

tickMaxPriceAbsolute.description:
Maximum allowed absolute price change from the tick reference price 
Cannot be used together with `tickMaxPricePercentage`

tradingTypes.description:
Optional 
If not defined, priceBand applies for all trade types 
Allowed values: 
- CLOB 
- RFQ 
- Trade Types on Trade Entry

applicablePriceBands.description:
Optional 
If not configured, all price bands apply for all trade types. 
If configured, only the specified price bands apply for specified trade types.

underlyingInstrumentId.description:
Applicable if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) OR K (Strategies) 
Underlying instrument id.

strategyType.description: Mandatory if Category = K (Strategies) Strategy type

legsInstrumentsIds.description:
Mandatory if Category = K (Strategies) 
Array of instrument ids of that strategy - sorted so that leg1 is the first leg2 second etc. 
- On spread: 2 legs are required 
- On strips: 2 ≤ number of legs < 24

leg1ReferencePrice.description:
Optional 
Mandatory if Category = K (Strategies) AND strategyType=SPREAD. Optional if Category = K (Strategies) AND strategyType!=SPREAD 
Leg1 reference price

legPriceBands.description:
Optional 
Applicable only if Category = K (Strategies)
No — default if not sent
Yes — System prevents breaching price bands of legs during trades allocation and order entry

bidAskTickRefPrice.description:
Optional 
true = System considers best Bid/best Ask when calculating tickReferencePrice 
false = best Bid/best Ask is not considered (deafult)

coupon.description:
Only if Category = D(Dept instruments) 
The coupon of the bonds

periodOfCoupon.description:
Only if Category = D(Dept instruments) 
The period of the coupon 
optional values: 
ANNUAL 
SEMI_ANNUAL 
QUARTERLY

accrualConvention.description:
Only if Category = D(Dept instruments) 
The Convention calculate accrual of the coupon 
optinal values: 
ACT_ACT 
NO_ACCRUAL 
ACT_360 
_30E_360 
ACT_365

maturityDate.description:
Only if Category = D(Dept instruments) 
The maturity date of the bonds 
format YYYY-MM-DD

deliveryStartDate.description:
Delivery start date. 
Applicable if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) or K (Strategies) 
format YYYY-MM-DD

deliveryEndDate.description:
Delivery end date. 
Applicable if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) or K (Strategies) 
format YYYY-MM-DD

settlementDate.description:
Optional 
Applicable if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) or K (Strategies) 
Settlement date. format YYYY-MM-DD

periodCode.description:
Optional 
Applicable if Category = O (option) OR F (Futures) or K (Strategies) 
Period code

tradingModels.description:
Allowed values in the array
CLOB — instrument supports Central Limit Order Book
RFQ — instrument supports Request For Quote

maxRFQExpireTimePeriod.description:
Required if tradingModels has ‘RFQ’. 
Max allowed expiry time for an RFQ in seconds. 
Note: this is the time to maintain RFQ without expiration due to timeout.

allegedTradeExpireTimePeriod.description:
`(CHANGED v1.51.0)` 
Active Alleged Trades are canceled by the system If configured, after allegedTradeExpireTimePeriod, 
If not configured, at EOD

type.description:
trade entry type 
One of the below values the: 
Refer Trade Entry/type field for the values; all values are supported.

amount.description: Min amount for Trade Entry for the selected type

minQuantityTradeEntries.description: the minimum quntity for trade entry

InstrumentCategory.description:
Instrument category.
E — Equities
C — Collective investment vehicles
D — Debt instruments
R — Entitlement (rights)
O — Listed options
F — Futures
S — Swaps
H — Non-listed and complex listed options
I — Spot
J — Forwards
K — Strategies
L — Financing
T — Referential instruments
M — Others (miscellaneous)

id.description:
Instrument Group ID 
(Not part of the request body of Create Instrument Group)

name.description:
Name of the Group 
May contain values that are not unique 
Max length - 30 chars and can contain ASCII (32 -126)

InstrumentSubCategory.description:
Instrument sub category. Valid values depend on the selected category:
F — Foreign exchange (category: Spot)
T — Commodities (category: Spot)
F — Financial futures (category: Futures)
C — Commodities futures (category: Futures)
N — Binary Event Contract 
Note: Yes instruments only (category: Futures)
C — Call options (category: Listed options)
P — Put options (category: Listed options)
M — Other (miscellaneous) (category: Listed options)
B — Bonds (category: Debt instruments)

InstrumentUnderlyingAssets.description:
Instrument underlying assets type. Applicability depends on category/subCategory:
A — Agriculture
J — Energy
K — Metals
N — Environmental
P — Polypropylene Products
S — Fertilizer
T — Paper
M — Others (Miscellaneous)
X — Not Set
E — Extraction Resources
I — Industrial Products
S — Services
H — Generated Resources
M — Others
B — Baskets
S — Stock-Equities
D — Debt Instruments
C — Currencies
I — Indices
O — Options
F — Futures
W — Swaps
N — Interest Rates
V — Stock Dividend
T — Commodities
F — Fixed Rate
Z — Zero Rate/Discounted
V — Variable
C — Cash Payment
K — Payment in Kind

id.description: MP ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update MP)

name.description:
Unique MP Name 
Max length = 30 and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

longName.description:
Long Name 
Max length = 100 and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

description.description:
Description 
Max length = 100 and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

status.description:
Active — can trade.
Blocked — can't send any order RFQ, Quote and Alleged Trade command

selfTradePrevention.description:
Determines whether self Trade Prevention is allowed for this MP or not. 
If not sent then it is considered as false

selfTradeAction.description:
One of the below values: 
- CancelIncomingOrder 
- CancelRestingOrder 
- CancelBothOrders 
If not configured→ STP will work only on order level

lockedInMpIds.description:
A list of mpIds the MP can designate as counterparty when creating locked-in trades reports 
[0] = All MPs (All = current and future MPs)

allegedMpIds.description:
Optional 
A list of mpIds the MP can designate as counterparty when creating alleged trades reports & alleged transfers 
[0] = All MPs (All = current and future MPs)

thirdPartyLockedInMpIds.description:
Optional 
A list of mpId pairs the MP can designate as buy MP and sell MP when creating locked-in trades reports as a third-party MP reporting the trade. 
[0,0] = All MPs with All MPs 
[0, 100] = mpId 100 with All MPs

permissions.description: Permissions grantted for the apiKey. A table of available values is listed below

cancelOnDisconncet.description:
true - when there is disconnection all resting orders, active Quotes and active Alleged Trades are canceled 
Note: The field can set to be true only when "market-service:market:place_order" OR "market-service:market:submit_rfq_quotes" OR "market-service:market:create_trade_report" permission is included

allowedInstrumentGroupIds.description:
Optional 
This apiKey grants access only to the instruments in these groups. 
Note: Only one of allowedInstruments and allowedInstrumentGroupIds is allowed

accountId.description:
Defines the Exchange Account the API key has access/visibility to. 
If not defined, the API Key has access/visibility to all accounts.

id.description: MP Group ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update MP Group)

name.description: Unique MP Group Name, max length =50 charecters and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

compId.description: Unique compId to be configured on FIX connection for that MP Group. Max length = 16 and ASCII (32 -126) chars only

mpIds.description: List of MPs that this group contains, for all MPs, do not send this property.

MpGroupApiKey.description: API key attached to an MP Group. Structurally identical to MpApiKey, with two differences: it is scoped to an MP Group (groupId path parameter) instead of a single MP (mpId), and the set of allowed permissions is a subset of the MP API key permissions.

id.description: Tick Size ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update TickSize)

name.description:
Unique TickSize name 
Max length is 30

price.description:
Price to- use the price when order price ≤ to this price and > from previous price (this array need to be sorted). 
If empty this will apply to all order prices above the previous price.

tickSize.description: Tick size to be used for current price level

items.description:
list of items 
(NEW v1.40.0) 
items are stored after sorting by their prices(ascending order)

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Authenticate with the email and password provided by the Exberry team to receive a JWT token. Use it as a Bearer token on every other request. Regenerate before expiresIn elapses.

tags.name: Instruments
tags.description:
Instruments API allows you to:
- Get single instrument details
- Get the list of all instruments with their details
- Create an instrument
- Update existing instruments
- Archive an instrument that is no longer in use
Samples for both mandatory fields only and all fields can be found in the `Get Instrument` section

tags.name: Instrument Groups
tags.description:
Instrument groups allows users to manage instrument grouping with few levels of hierarchy.
This is internal feature allow exchange operators to group instruments as needed (this information is not published to participants/ traders anywhere).
After creating the groups, an instrument can be added and removed to/ from a group.
Instrument Groups API allows you to:
- Get single Instrument Group details
- Get the list of all instrument Groups with their details
- Create an Instrument Group
- Update an existing Instrument Group

tags.name: Calendars
tags.description:
Calendars API allows you to:
- Get single calendar details
- Get the list of all calendars with their details
- Create a calendar
- Update existing calendars
- Trigger EOD for a specific calendar

tags.name: MPs
tags.description:
MP API allows you to:
- Get single MP details
- Get the list of all MPs with their details
- Create MP
- Update existing MP
MP Object

tags.name: MP Groups
tags.description:
MP Group allows you to configure read only access for group of market participants (or all of them).
MP Groups API allows you to:
- Get single MP Group details
- Get the list of all MP Groups with their details
- Create MP Group
- Update existing MP Group
MP Group Object

tags.name: CBR
tags.description:
Circuit Breaker Rules (CBR) API allows you to:
- Get single CBR details
- Get the list of all CBRs with their details
- Create a CBR
- Update existing CBRs

tags.name: Tick Size
tags.description:
TickSize API allows you to:
- Get single TickSize details
- Get the list of all TickSizes with their details
- Create a TickSize
- Update existing TickSize

tags.name: Operations
tags.description: Operations API allows you to send operational command

post.description:
Exchanges email + password for a JWT bearer token.

#### Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 2 | Wrong email or password |
| 10000 | Please login to Admin Application before using API |

token.description: Token to be used for any API call

post.description:
This API allows to create a new instrument. ID will be returned in the response, this ID is the unique identifier of the instrument and should be used when sending update commands. Note: this ID can be retrieved via Get Instruments API. #### Create Instrument- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 101 | \[FieldName\] must be less than or equal to maxLong/10^ fieldPrecision | | 101 | \[FieldName\] must be more than or equal to minLong/10^ fieldPrecision | | 101 | Precision of \[Fieldname\] is greater than \[quantityPrecision\] | | 101 | Precision of \[Fieldname\] is greater than \[pricePrecision\] | | 101 | Precision of quantityIncrement must be \[quantityPrecision\] (quantityPrecision) | | 101 | minQuantity should be less than maxQuantity or equal to it | | 101 | minPrice should be less than maxPrice or equal to it | | 101 | maxQuantity should be less than maxDepth or equal to it | | 101 | Stop Date must be after Start Date | | 101 | Stop date must be in future | | 101 | underlyingInstrumentId/legsInstrumentIds not found | | 101 | Legs \[price/quantity\] precision must be equal to strategy price precision | | 102 | symbol already exists for id: \[instrumentId\] | | 104 | Implied orders are not allowed for \[leg/strategySymbol\] while included in the \[CBRName\] CBR | | 105 | Legs \[price/quantity\] precision must be equal to strategy price precision | | 105 | Strategies are not allowed as legs | | 105 | Legs and Strategy calendars must be the same

Note: this validation relevant only for active instruments | | 2603 | \[fieldName\] of \[fieldValue\] is not allowed as instrument price precision is \[instrumentPricePrecision\] | | 2605 | \[FieldName\] must be less than maxLong/10^ pricePrecision | | 10001 | Permission denied |

get.description:
This API allows to get a list of all instruments with their details.

#### Get Instruments- Fields

**Example for derivativeInstrumentCategory instrument -**

- InstrumentId: 1,
    
- InstrumentId: 2,
    
- InstrumentId: 3,
    
- InstrumentId: 4,
    
- InstrumentId: 10, underlyingInstrumentId: 1, category: Futures, status: active
    
- InstrumentId: 11, underlyingInstrumentId: 2, category: Futures, status: disabled
    
- InstrumentId: 12, underlyingInstrumentId: 3, category: Spot, status: active
    
- InstrumentId: 13, underlyingInstrumentId: 4, category: Spot, status: disabled
    

When Derivative Instrument Category = Futures → InstrumentId:1 is returned.

When Derivative Instrument Category = Spot →InstrumentId:3 is returned.

#### Suffix & Prefix

| Pattern Type | Description | Sample |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Contains the word "X" | Find any description/ symbols that contains "X" | X |
| Starts with "X" | Find any description/ symbols that start with "X" | X\* |
| Ends with "X" | Find any description/ symbol that ends with "X" | \*X |
| Equal to 'X' | Find any description/ symbol that = "X" | 'X' |

#### NEW v1.30.0 Get Instrument- Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 102 | Single category should be sent |
| 102 | Wrong Sub Category |
| 102 | Strategies category should be sent |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
free text search by symbol 
See the Suffix/Prefix table below

parameters.name: description
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
free text search by description 
See the Suffix/Prefix table below

parameters.name: calendarId
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
search by calendarId

parameters.name: tickSizeTableId
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
search by tickSizeTableId

parameters.name: tradingModel
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
search by tradingModel 
- CLOB 
- RFQ

parameters.name: startDateFrom
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search for the instrument that startdate ≥ dateFrom. 
Format:YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[.SSS] 
For instruments without startDate, send - `0000-01-01T00:00`

parameters.name: startDateTo
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search for the instrument that startdate < dateTo. Format:YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[.SSS]

parameters.name: stopDateFrom
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search for the instrument that stopdate ≥ dateFrom. Format:YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[.SSS] 
For instruments without stopDate, send - `0000-01-01T00:00`

parameters.name: stopDateTo
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search for the instrument that stopdate < dateTo. Format:YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[.SSS]

parameters.name: product
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search by underlyingInstrumentId for instruments without product - send 0

parameters.name: derivativeInstrumentCategory
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search instruments that has an underling instrument and is in this category & active 
For example see below 
for values see in the Category List - here

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search by the list of statuses 
- ACTIVE 
- DISABLED 
- ARCHIVED

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search by catagory. 
for values see in the Category List - here

parameters.name: subCategory
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search by sub-catagory. 
for values see in the SubCategory List - here

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Search by strategy. 
- SPREAD 
- STRIP

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
Which record to start send from 
If nothing was sent default is 0 (=first record)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
How many records to include in each page 
If nothing was sent default is 10,000

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
`Optional` 
object with 2 parameters: 
- field (String) = Symbol (case-insensitive) 
- direction (Asc, Desc) 
If nothing or invalid field was sent the default is [Symbol, Asc]

put.description:
This API allows to update existing instrument.

#### Update Instrument- Error Codes

All validations in create instrument and the below

All validations in create instrument and the below

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |
| 101 | \[FieldName\] not found |
| 101 | \[Start Date/~~Stop Date~~ Removed v1.25 ~~/~~ Category\] can’t be changed after start date |
| 101 | Category can’t be changed for instrument that is underlying instrument |
| 101 | \[FieldName\] can’t be changed for strategy related instrument |
| 101 | \[FieldName\] can’t be changed after elapsed |
| 105 | Mass cancellation must be done before disabling the instrument |
| 2402 | Mass cancellation must be done before changing quantityPrecision |
| 2402 | Mass cancellation must be done before changing pricePrecision |
| 10001 | Permission denied |

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Instrument ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Instrument)

get.description: This API allows to get a single instrument details.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Instrument ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Instrument)

post.description:
This API allows to archiving of existing instrument Please contact the Exberry team if you would like to enable it on your system. In order to support a large number of instruments we introduce the ability to archive instruments. This is an irreversible operation that should happen only in case the instrument is no longer in use permanently. When an instrument is archived, you can still access historical financial information about it, including Orders, Order events, Trades, and Ops reports. You can also create a new instrument with the same symbol, but the archived instrument is no longer tradable. Archived instruments will be served only in those places: - Reporting - Orders - Orders event - Trades - Operations - Reporting API: - vN/exchange.reporting/mp/orders - vN/exchange.reporting/mp/trades In all other APIs, archived instruments will not be served. Prior to archiving an instrument, it must first be disabled, in case this instrument is a leg of a strategy, the parent must be archived prior to its legs. #### Archive Instrument- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | Exchange is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | Disable the instrument before attempting to archive it | | 106 | Archiving strategy \[parentSymbol\] is required | | 107
Note: This validation will only take effect after the first update for that instrument | This instrument is underlying instrument of \[Derivative Symbol\] | | 10001 | Permission denied |

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Instrument ID (Not part of request body in Create/ Update Instrument)

example.message: Disable the instrument before attempting to archive it

post.description:
This API allows to create a new instrument Group.

An id will be returned in the response, this id is the unique identifier of the instrument group and should be used when sending update commands.

Note: This id can be retrieved via Get Instrument Groups API.

#### Create Instrument Group - Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | System is unavailable |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |
| 101 | Number of hierarchy levels should be <= 3 |
| 101 | Not allowed |
| 10001 | Permission denied |

get.description: This API allows to get all instrument group details.

put.description:
This API allows to update existing instrument group.

#### Update Instrument Group - Error Codes

All validations in create instrument group

This API allows to create a new calendar. ID will be returned in the response, this ID is the unique identifier of the calendar and should be used when sending update commands. Note: this ID can be retrieved via Get Calendars API. #### Create Calendar- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | Holiday date should not be sent twice | | 101 | Wrong holiday close time | | 101 | name already exists | | `NEW v1.33.0`
101 | IOC TIF is allowed only for Equilibrium Price | | `NEW v1.35.0`
101 | AutoResume auction is required by \[CBRName\] CBR | | 10001 | Permission denied | | 10005 | Not found | | 20000 | Calendar Name already exists | | 20001 | Wrong format | | 20002 | Wrong format | #### Auction - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | Auction start must be after previous auction end | | 101 | randomEndTime / 2 must be less than duration | | 101 | Wrong duration and randomEndTime | | \[REMOVED 1.42.0\]~~
101~~ | ~~One side must contain single mpId~~ | | 101 | \[fieldName\] is relevant only for timeBased auction | | 101 | Only single auction can be configured with \[fieldName\] trigger | | 101 | overrideReferencePrice can be True only for equilibrium price algorithm | | 101 | AutoResume auction is required by \[CBRName\] CBR | | 101 | IOC TIF is allowed only for Equilibrium Price | | `NEW v1.38.0`
102 | The auction duration in \[calendar\] is shorter than the minimum allowed duration | | 10001 | Permission denied | | 10005 | Not found |

This API allows to create a new apiKey for a specific MP. As a response system will generate pair of apiKey and secret key. For your security, your API Secret will only be returned at the time it is created. If you lose it, you will need to delete your API key and set up a new one. #### Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | label already exists | | `(REMOVED v1.43.0)`
60002 | Allowed and blocked instruments/`(NEW v1.42.0)` instrumentGroups can not be filled for a single apiKey |

This API allows to create a new CBR. ID will be returned in the response, this ID is the unique identifier of the CBR and should be used when sending update commands. Note: this ID can be retrieved via Get CBRs API. #### Create CBR - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | `NEW v1.42.0`
100 | Only one of instrumentIds and instrumentGroupIds is allowed | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | name already exists | | `REMOVED v1.37`
`NEW v1.35.0` ~~104~~ | ~~The calendar was copied successfully with the auctions from original calendar~~ | | `NEW v1.35.0` 104 | only Resume Auction is allowed for this CBR type | | `NEW v1.35.0` 104 | AutoResume auction needs to be configured with override reference price | | 105 | Priority \[value\] already exists | | `NEW v1.58.0` 2001 | You can send up to 750 instruments per request | | 10001 | Permission denied |

post.description:
Place Order API allows you to place an order for mp For more details about place order - see [here](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) #### Place order- Fields | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Int String | Instrument id for the order | | mpId | Int String | mpId | | mpOrderId | Long | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | orderType | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | side | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | quantity | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | price `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | timeInForce `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | expiryDate `optional` | UTC Time[](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | expiryDay `optional` | String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | minQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | stopPrice `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | stpAction `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | accountType `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | parties `optional` | \[\] Object | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | displayMethod `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | displayQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | displayLowQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | displayHighQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | | `(NEW v1.50.0)`
metadata `optional` | String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#placeorder) | #### Place Order - Error Codes 1. Validations: same as [place order](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1N_7UEglP8s6hNV9ZeE5MN2NWdpJw-phRbo8Pk3qW2Go/edit#bookmark=id.gsv875ykqvln), with the below changes: 1. Skip validations 1007 & 1008 & 1011 (Authentication related) 2. Additional validation: | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 10001 | Permission denied |

Cancel Order API allows you to cancel order #### Cancel order - Fields | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Int | Instrument id for the trade | | orderId | Int | the Id of the order we want to cancel | | cancelReason | Enum | `Optional`
Cancellation reason. (populated in the cancellation events)

Allowed values:
CancelOrderOnBehalf (default if nothing is sent)
CancelRequest
CorporateAction | #### Cancel Order - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during crossing | | 102 | Instrument trading is not allowed | | 102 | Market is closed | | 102 | Instrument trading is halt | | 101 | Not allowed | | 108 | Cancellation of implied order is not allowed | | 10001 | Permission denied |

Replace Order API allows you to replace an order for mp.

For more details about replace order - see [here](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder)

#### Replace order- Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| instrumentId | Int String | Original order instrument Id |
| orderId | Int String | Exchange order ID to be replaced |
| quantity | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| price `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| timeInForce `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| expiryDate `optional` | UTC Time | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| expiryDay `optional` | String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| minQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| stopPrice `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| stpAction `optional` | Enum | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| displayQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| displayLowQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| displayHighQuantity `optional` | Decimal String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |
| `(NEW v1.51.0)`metadata `optional` | String | Similar to the [trading API](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder) |

#### Relace Order - Error Codes

1. Validations: same as [replaceOrder](https://docs.exberry.io/ws/trading-api#replaceorder), with the below changes:
    
    1. Skip validations 1007 & 1008 &1011 (Authentication related)
        
2. Additional validations:
    

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 10001 | Permission denied |
| 108 | Changes to the implied orders are not allowed |

Mass Cancel API allows you to mass cancel pending activity per request criteria: Request Parameters | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Stringfied number | Instrument ID to mass cancel the orders | | `Removed v1.30.0`
~~comment~~ | ~~String~~ | ~~Free text comment~~ | | mpId | Stringfied number | `Optional`
MP ID to mass cancel the target entities
Not sending this parameter will mass cancel for all MPs | | targetParties | \[\]TargetParty objects | `Optional`

targetParties to target the mass cancel orders.
If multiple targetParty objects are in a request, the system cancels orders with all targetParties.
For example:
Order1 has party1
Order2 has party1 and party2
Order3 has party1, party2 and party3
Order4 has no parties
A mass cancel request with targetParties(party1 and party2) will cancel order 2 and 3.

`CHANGED v1.46.0` Note: For accounts: id=accountId, ~~source=D, role=1001~~ source=P, role=24 | | `(NEW 1.30.0)` targetEntities | \[\] Enum | `Optional`

The system cancels the type of active entities mentioned in the request.

Array of below values:
Order - cancels all orders
RFQ - cancels all RFQs and all active quotes belong to them
Quote - cancels all Quotes
`(NEW v1.31.0)` AllegedTrade - cancels all Alleged Trades

If not exist, system considers as targetEntities = \[‘Order’\] | | `(NEW 1.31.0)` targetScope | Enum | The field specifies the scope the mass cancel request applies to

Allowed values:
Instrument - cancel scope is a single instrument
All - cancel scope is all instruments

If not sent “Instrument” is considered. | | `(NEW 1.32.0)`
cancelReason | Enum | Optional
Cancellation reason. (populated in the cancellation events)

Allowed values:
MassCancelOnBehalf (default if nothing is sent)
MassCancelRequest
CorporateAction | ##### NEW **TargetParty specification:** | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | string | TargetParty id | | source | char | TargetParty source | | role | int | TargetParty role | #### Mass Cancel- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 2001 | Wrong action | | 2001 | Wrong instrumentIds | | 2401 | Wrong instrumentId: \[instrumentId\] | | 403 | Permission denied |

Trade Entry API allows you to capture trade that was done out of the order book #### Trade Entry- Fields | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Int | Instrument id for the trade | | type | eNum | Type of the trade entry

See the table below for supported values | | price | Decimal | Trade price | | quantity | Decimal | Trade quantity | | buy | Side obj | See below | | sell | Side obj | See below | ##### Side obj- | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | mpId | Int | mpId | | accountType | eNum | Client/House | | parties | \[\]obj | list of parties obj | ###### parties obj- | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | id | string(20) | The Id of the party | | source | Char | Id source = D | | role | int | Id role = 38 | ##### Supported values for the type field | Trade Type | Description | **FIX** TrdType(828) | | --- | --- | --- | | Block | Block trade | 1 | | Other | Other | 99 | | EFRP | Exchange for Related Position | 2 | | ECRP | Exchange of Contracts for Related Positions | 101 | | EFP | Exchange for Physical | 102 | | `(NEW v1.47.0)` Reversal | Trade Reversal | 103 | | Transfer | Transfer | 3 | | EFS | Exchange for Swap | 12 | | EFR | Exchange for Risk | 11 | | EOO | Exchange of Options for Options | 14 | | OPNT | Over the Counter Privately Negotiated Trades | 22 | | SFF | Substitution of Futures for Forwards | 23 | | OptionExercise | Option exercise | 45 | | OTCLNOFS | OTC / Large Notional Off Facility Swap | 54 | | EBF | Exchange Basis Facility | 55 | | Netted | Netted trade | 57 | | BlockSwap | Block swap trade | 58 | | CreditEvent | Credit event trade | 59 | | SuccessionEvent | Succession event trade | 60 | #### Trade Entry- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 100 | Trade \[FieldName\] must be less than \[maxLong/10^ fieldPrecision | | 100 | Trade \[FieldName\] must be above than \[minLong/10^ fieldPrecision\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | Currently not allowed | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during auction call | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during crossing | | 102 | Instrument trading is not allowed | | 102 | Price precision is\[PricePrecision\] | | 102 | Quantity precision is\[QuantityPrecision\] | | 102 | Minimum trade quantity is \[MinQuantity\] | | 102 | MinQuantity = max( minQuantity,
minQuantityTradeEntry.amount) | | 102 | Maximum trade quantity is \[MaxQuantity\] | | 102 | Quantity increment is \[quantityIncrement\] | | 102 | Instrument trading is halted | | 102 | Price breaches \[FieldName\] of \[FieldValue\] (NEW v1.56.0) \[Optional Text\]

_(NEW v1.56.0)
_\[Optional Text\] = Price Band \[Value\]\[Type\], Reference Price: \[RefPrice\] , returned only in case of dynamic/ static price bands and not in case of leg price bands or instrument min/ max price bands | | 102 | Maximum value is \[maxOrderValue\] | | 102 | Price tick size is \[tickSize\] | | 102 | Trading is not allowed | | 102 | Missing position account | | `(NEW v1.56.0)`
113 | Not supported | | 10001 | Permission denied |

Trade Cancellation API allows you to cancel a trade

#### Trade Cancellation- Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| instrumentId | int String | Instrument ID to cancel the orders |
| matchId | int String | Trade Id to cancel |

#### Trade Cancellation- Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | System is unavailable |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |
| 101 | \[FieldName\] not found |
| 102 | Currently not allowed |
| 102 | Instrument trading is not allowed |
| 105 | Cancelation failed, trade is already canceled or from previous days or is leg trade |
| 10001 | Permission denied |

example.message: Cancelation failed, trade is already canceled or from previous days

post.description:
Trade Adjustment allows to change the price of a trade #### Trade Adjustment- Fields | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Int | Instrument ID to cancel the orders | | tradeId | Int | Trade Id | | newPrice | Decimal | New price of the trade | | excludedPriceBands | \[ \] Enum | Optional
Excluded prices from Price Bands.

Allowed values:
INSTRUMENT_MIN_MAX
DAILY_MIN_MAX
TICK_MIN_MAX | #### Trade Adjustment- Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] not found | | 102 | Currently not allowed | | 102 | Instrument trading is not allowed | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during auction call | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during crossing | | 102 | Price precision is\[PricePrecision\] | | 102 | Quantity precision is\[QuantityPrecision\] | | 102 | Instrument trading is halted | | 102 | Price breaches \[FieldName\] of \[FieldValue\] | | 102 | Maximum value is \[maxOrderValue\] | | 102 | Price tick size is \[tickSize\] | | 102 | Trading is not allowed | | 102 | Request failed, strategy leg configuration has changed | | 105 | Request failed, trade is already canceled or from previous days or is leg trade | | 105 | Implied order trades can’t be adjusted | | 111 | Not allowed when LEDGER is enabled | | 10001 | Permission denied |

example.message: Request failed, trade is already canceled or from previous days or is leg trade

post.description:
Apply Cash Distribution API allows you to adjust active orders and current market data fields(not historical) in case of a Corporate Actions that results cash distributions (such as cash dividend). #### **Request Parameters:** | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Stringified number | Instrument ID to apply the cash distribution | | amount | Stringified Decimal | Cash Amount per share

Maximum 10 decimal places | #### **Response:** | **Field** | Description | | --- | --- | | numberOfOrders | The total number of active/suspended orders for the instrument before applying the corporate action | | numberOfNewOrders | The number of new orders generated for the corporate action | | numberOfCancelledOrders | The number of cancelled orders for the corporate action | #### Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 10001 | Permission denied | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 100 | \[FieldName\] must be ≤ \[maxLong/10^ fieldPrecision\] | | 100 | \[FieldName\] must be ≥ \[minLong/10^ fieldPrecision\] | | 102 | Instrument trading is not allowed | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during auction call | | 102 | Trading is not allowed during crossing | | 102 | Instrument should be halted when market is open | | 102 | Apply Cash Distribution is not allowed when there is no Closing Price |

Apply Security Distribution API allows you to adjust active orders and current market data fields(not historical) in case of a Corporate Actions that results security distributions (such as stock dividend). #### Request Parameters | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Stringified number | Instrument ID to apply the cash distribution | | ratioOld | Stringified Decimal | ratioOld of the stock dividend/forward split
Maximum 10 decimal places

If 1 new share is issued for each 2 shares held, ratioOld=2 and ratioNew=1. (European Convention) | | ratioNew | Stringified Decimal | ratioNew of the stock dividend/forward split
Maximum 10 decimal places

See description of ratioOld for more details | | quantityRounding | Enum | Rounding method applicable to fractional quantities
Allowed values:
DOWN
UP
NONE | #### **Response:** Same as Apply Cash Distribution #### Error Codes Same as Apply Cash Distribution with below exceptions | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 102 | Apply Cash Distribution is not allowed when there is no Closing Price

Not applicable to Apply Security Distribution |

get.description:
Get Candle Adjustments API allows you to retrieve the historical market data adjustment factors related to Corporate Actions at instrument level. #### Request Parameters | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | dateFrom | Stringified Date | Optional
Search for the CAs that exDate ≥ dateFrom. Format:YYYY-MM-DD | | dateTo | Stringified Date | Optional
Search for the CAs that exDate < dateTo
Format: YYYY-MM-DD | | instrumentId | Stringified Int | Optional

Instrument Id | | orderBy | Enum | Optional
object with 2 parameters:
field (String)
direction (Asc, Desc) direction

If nothing or invalid field was sent the default is \[exDate, Desc\] | | limit | Stringified Int | Optional
How many records to include in each page
If nothing was sent default is 25
Max value = 100 | | offset | Stringified Int | Optional
Which record to start send from (if you want page 3 and there are 50 records per page offset should be 150)
If nothing was sent default is 0 (=first record) | #### **Response:** | Attribute | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | count | Stringified Int | total number of records based on filter | | List of adjustmentFactors | | | | instrumentId | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | | exDate | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | | price | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | | quantity | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | Same as Update Candle Adjustments | #### Error Codes Same as Update Candle Adjustments

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description:
Search for the CAs that exDate ≥ dateFrom. 
Format:YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description:
Search for the CAs that exDate < dateTo
Format: YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: instrumentId
parameters.description: Instrument Id

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
object with 2 parameters:
field (String)
direction (Asc, Desc) direction
If nothing or invalid field was sent the default is [exDate, Desc]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: How many records to include in each page

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
Which record to start send from (if you want page 3 and there are 50 records per page offset should be 150)
If nothing was sent default is 0 (=first record)

put.description:
Update Candle Adjustments API allows you to create/update the historical market data adjustment factors related to Corporate Actions at instrument level. #### Request Parameters | Attribute | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Stringified number | Instrument ID to apply the cash distribution | | exDate | Stringified Date | Ex-date of the corporate action
format YYYY-MM-DD | | price | Stringified Decimal | Optional

The adjustment factor applicable to price related market data values prior to the exDate

price or quantity is required | | quantity | Stringified Decimal | Optional

The adjustment factor applicable to quantity related market data values prior to the exDate

price or quantity is required | #### **Response:** No response body #### Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 10001 | Permission denied | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | \[FieldName\] must be ≤ \[maxLong/10^ fieldPrecision\] |

delete.description:
Delete Candle Adjustments API allows you to delete the historical market data adjustment factors related to Corporate Actions at instrument level. #### Request Parameters | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | Stringified number | Instrument ID to apply the cash distribution | | exDate | Stringified Date | Optional

Ex-date of the corporate action
format YYYY-MM-DD

If not exist, delete all adjustment factors of the instrument | #### **Response:** No response body #### Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | System is unavailable | | 10001 | Permission denied | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |

post.description:
Set Settlement Price API allows you to set settlement price for an instrument #### Set Settlement Prices - Request Fields | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentId | string | Instrument ID to set a Settlement Price | | price | string | The price to set | | updateReferencePrice
`Optional` | Boolean | Update the reference price with the settlement price
default is True | #### Set Settlement Prices - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 1 | Exchange is unavailable | | 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] | | 101 | instrumentId not found | | 102 | Currently not allowed | | 10001 | Permission denied |

Set Tick Reference Price API allows you to set Tick Reference Price for an instrument

#### Tick Reference Price - Request Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| instrumentId | string | Instrument ID to set a Tick Reference Price |
| price | string | Tick Reference Price to set |

#### Tick Reference Price - Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Exchange is unavailable |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |
| 101 | instrumentId not found |
| 10001 | Permission denied |

Trading Halt API allows you to halt the trading for a: - List of instrument - List of instrument groups - All instruments - Multiple filtered instruments, see [here](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/6229811/TzCV3jcq#c1b23770-4cc1-41b0-a035-15f3e280bbe4) for more details | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentIds | \[\] Stringified numbers | `Optional`
List of instruments to halt
Note: not sending this parameter will halt the trading for all instruments | | instrumentGroupIds | \[\] Stringified numbers | `Optional`List of instrument groups to halt | | `(NEW v1.57.0)`
scheduleHaltTime | Unix time (sec) | `Optional`
When to halt the trading
If this is not sent, the halt will be immediate | | scheduleResumeTime | Unix time (sec) | `Optional`
When to resume trading | | scheduleResumeTimeInterval | Int String | `Optional`
When to resume trading in minutes Interval | #### Schedule Halt and Resume CHANGED 1.57.0 1. When the request sets a halt, it is allowed to specify halt at explicit time as well as the explicit resumption time. If not defind, action is immediate. When scheduling resume, a time gap extension instead of an explicit time can be defined. 2. Examples: 1. Now it's 14:00 and a user clicks on halt with a time interval of 10 minutes, trading will resume at 14:10. 2. In addition to the previous example, at 14:07 the user again clicks on halt with a time interval of 5 minutes, trading will resume at 14:15 (14:10 plus the 5 minutes from the latest request). **Behavior Summary** 1. Scheduled Resume can be triggered by one of the below: 1. CBR: Triggered by CBR 2. Regular: Triggered by operator (via time interval or explicit time) | New Request | Existing Timers | Behavior | | --- | --- | --- | | Halt Without Scheduled Resume

OR
Halt & Scheduled Resume With Explicit Time

OR
Immediate Resume

OR
Scheduled Halt

OR
Scheduled Halt & Scheduled Resume | Scheduled Halt
with/ without any Scheduled Resume

OR
Scheduled Resume | Drop existing schedule and apply the new command | | Halt & Scheduled Resume with Time Interval | Scheduled Resume Regular | Interval of the new request will be added to the original Scheduled Resume time. | | Halt & Scheduled Resume with Time Interval | Scheduled CBR Resume
OR
Scheduled Halt
with/ without any Scheduled Resume | Drop existing schedule and apply the new command | | CBR Halt with/without Resume | Scheduled Halt
with/ without Scheduled Resume
OR
Scheduled Resume | Doesn’t drop existing scheduled halts/resumes and halts immediately and schedule new resume (if applicable) | #### Trading Halt - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 2001 | Wrong action | | 2001 | Wrong instrumentIds | | 2002 | \[FieldName\] must be in future | | 2003 | Only one of scheduleResumeTime and scheduleResumeTimeInterval is allowed | | 2401 | Wrong instrumentId: \[instrumentId\] | | 2401 | Wrong instrumentGroupIds: \[instrumentGroupId\] | | 403 | Permission denied | | 201 | You can send up to 750 instruments per request |

Trading Resume API allows you to resume the trading for a: - List of instrument - List of instrument groups - All instruments - Multiple filtered instruments, see [here](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/6229811/TzCV3jcq#c1b23770-4cc1-41b0-a035-15f3e280bbe4) for more details | | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | instrumentIds | \[\] Stringified numbers | `Optional`
List of instruments to resume
Note: not sending this parameter will resume the trading for all instruments | | instrumentGroupIds | \[\] Stringified numbers | `Optional`List of instrument groups to resume | #### Trading Resume - Error Codes | Code | Message | | --- | --- | | 2001 | Wrong action | | 2001 | Wrong instrumentIds | | 2001 | Wrong \[FieldName\] | | 2401 | Wrong instrumentId: \[instrumentId\] | | 2401 | Wrong instrumentGroupIds: \[instrumentGroupId\] | | 403 | Permission denied | | 201 | You can send up to 750 instruments per request |

New v1.50.0

Set News API allows you to submit news to the system

#### Set News - Request Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | int | New id, uniqueness is not validated by the system; it is the client’s responsibility to maintain it |
| message | string | News message |

#### Set News - Error Codes

| Code | Message |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Exchange is unavailable |
| 100 | Missing or invalid parameter: \[FieldName\] |
| 10001 | Permission denied |
